nodes.name: Meta Verify GET1
nodes.notes: Step 0: Meta calls GET to verify the webhook endpoint is reachable before enabling message delivery. Must respond with hub.challenge.

nodes.name: Verify Token Gate1
nodes.notes: Checks hub.mode == subscribe AND hub.verify_token matches env secret. Prevents unauthorized webhook registrations.

nodes.name: ACK: hub.challenge1
nodes.notes: Returns hub.challenge as plain text. Meta requires this exact response to activate the webhook.

nodes.name: Business Registered?1
nodes.notes: If business not found (404), drop the message silently. Phone number not registered on FlowCore.

nodes.name: Build Execution Context1
nodes.notes: Step 4: Merges resolved business metadata (name, branding, catalog, config) with customer message into a unified execution context for all downstream nodes.

nodes.name: Resolve Active Session1
nodes.notes: Step 5: Checks if an active non-terminal session exists for this (business_id, customer_phone) pair. Returns 404 if none — triggers session creation.

nodes.name: Create Session1
nodes.notes: Step 6a: Creates a new FlowCore traversal session when no active session exists. FlowCore auto-binds to the active workflow version for the business.

nodes.name: Resolve Session ID1
nodes.notes: Step 7: Picks the session_id from whichever path succeeded (existing session or new creation). Preserves full context for downstream dispatch.

nodes.name: FlowCore Dispatch1
nodes.notes: Step 8: THE CORE DISPATCH. Sends customer input into FlowCore's deterministic traversal engine. FlowCore owns ALL runtime decisions: FSM transitions, carry mutations, node execution, legality checks, replay guarantees.

nodes.name: Parse Dispatch Response1
nodes.notes: Step 9: Reads FlowCore's authoritative response. Extracts: messages, side_effects, fsm_state. Derives FSM-aware operational action buttons. Builds dynamic WhatsApp payload with business-specific branding.

nodes.name: Build WhatsApp Message1
nodes.notes: Step 11: Assembles final Meta API payload. Sends interactive button messages when FSM-aware actions are available (up to 3). Falls back to plain text otherwise. Business name and actions are fully dynamic.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Reply1
nodes.notes: Step 12: Sends message via Meta Cloud API v19.0. META_PHONE_NUMBER_ID and META_ACCESS_TOKEN injected from n8n environment variables — not hardcoded.

nodes.name: Log Orchestration Event1
nodes.notes: Logs orchestration events for debugging and monitoring. Does NOT duplicate FlowCore's deterministic replay journal.

nodes.name: Resolve Business1
nodes.notes: Step 3: Dynamically resolves which FlowCore business owns this Meta phone_number_id. Zero hardcoded business IDs — works for any business on the platform.

nodes.name: Extract Payload1
nodes.notes: Step 2: Extracts customer_phone, message_body, meta_phone_number_id from Meta's nested payload. Also handles interactive button replies mapping to operational action commands.

nodes.name: ACK Meta 200 OK1
nodes.notes: CRITICAL: Meta requires a 200 OK within 20 seconds of receiving a webhook or it will retry. Always ACK immediately, process asynchronously.

nodes.name: WhatsApp Message Ingest1
nodes.notes: Step 1: Receives all incoming WhatsApp messages from Meta Cloud API. POST endpoint handles real customer messages.

nodes.name: Execute Side Effects1
nodes.notes: Step 10: Generic side effects execution engine. Loops through FlowCore-decided side effects and routes to target adapters (Stripe, Courier, CRM, notifications) without branch explosion.

nodes.name: Get WhatsApp Config1
nodes.notes: Step 10.5: Fetches the authoritative WhatsApp credentials from FlowCore backend dynamically at runtime using the resolved business_id.
